name: recon-nmap
description: Network reconnaissance and security auditing using Nmap for port scanning, service enumeration, and vulnerability detection. Use when: (1) Conducting authorized network reconnaissance and asset discovery, (2) Enumerating network services and identifying running versions, (3) Detecting security vulnerabilities through NSE scripts, (4) Mapping network topology and firewall rules, (5) Performing compliance scanning for security assessments, (6) Validating network segmentation and access controls.
capabilities: Execute Nmap host discovery and ping sweeps on target networks
Perform port scanning with multiple techniques (TCP SYN, UDP, connect scans)
Run service version detection and OS fingerprinting on discovered hosts
Execute Nmap Scripting Engine (NSE) scripts for vulnerability detection
Generate scan reports in multiple formats (XML, grepable, normal output)
Apply firewall evasion techniques and timing optimizations
author: AgentSecOps
securitySummary: Documentation-only skill providing Nmap command examples and templates for authorized security testing. All 395 static findings are false positives - the scanner detected patterns in documentation describing legitimate security tooling, but the skill contains no executable code that performs scanning, network access, or credential handling.
valueStatement: Network reconnaissance is essential for security assessments but requires structured workflows to ensure thoroughness and compliance. This skill provides ready-to-use Nmap commands, workflow checklists, and integration templates for port scanning, service enumeration, and vulnerability detection.
limitations: Nmap must be installed on the system before using this skill
Network scanning requires explicit authorization from network owner
Skill provides guidance only; actual scanning is performed by Nmap
Results depend on network conditions and target configuration

useCases.title: Authorized penetration testing
useCases.description: Conduct network reconnaissance during authorized penetration tests with comprehensive Nmap workflows and compliance documentation

useCases.title: CI/CD security integration
useCases.description: Integrate Nmap scanning into CI/CD pipelines using provided GitHub Actions templates for continuous security validation

useCases.title: Network security assessments
useCases.description: Perform compliance scanning to validate network segmentation and identify security gaps against security frameworks

promptTemplates.title: Quick host discovery
promptTemplates.scenario: Find live hosts on a network
promptTemplates.prompt: Use Nmap to discover live hosts on network 192.168.1.0/24 using ping sweep and output results to live_hosts.txt

promptTemplates.title: Port enumeration
promptTemplates.prompt: Run Nmap service version detection (-sV) on all hosts in live_hosts.txt and save results to service_scan

promptTemplates.title: Vulnerability scan
promptTemplates.prompt: Execute NSE vulnerability scripts against targets to check for MS17-010, Heartbleed, and SSL vulnerabilities

promptTemplates.title: Comprehensive assessment
promptTemplates.prompt: Run comprehensive Nmap reconnaissance including host discovery, full port scan, OS detection, and vulnerability scripts on 10.0.1.0/24

bestPractices: Always verify written authorization before scanning any network or system
Start with host discovery before port scanning to reduce scan time and network impact
Use rate limiting (--max-rate) and timing templates (-T1 to -T4) to avoid overwhelming targets
antiPatterns: Running aggressive scans (-T5) without rate limiting on production networks
Scanning without first identifying the scope and obtaining proper authorization
Failing to document scan parameters and results for compliance and incident response

faq.question: Is Nmap installed by default?
faq.answer: No. Nmap must be installed separately on the system before using this skill. Install via package manager (apt, yum, brew).

faq.question: Can this skill scan any network?
faq.answer: Only scan networks you own or have explicit written authorization for. Unauthorized scanning may be illegal.

faq.question: Does this skill work offline?
faq.answer: Yes. All Nmap commands execute locally. External URLs in documentation are references only.

faq.question: Can I integrate with CI/CD pipelines?
faq.answer: Yes. The skill provides GitHub Actions templates for automated security scanning in pipelines.

faq.question: What scan types are supported?
faq.answer: TCP SYN (-sS), TCP Connect (-sT), UDP (-sU), service detection (-sV), OS detection (-O), and NSE scripts.

faq.question: How does this differ from vulnerability scanners?
faq.answer: Nmap provides network discovery and basic vulnerability detection via scripts. Full vulnerability scanners (Nessus, OpenVAS) offer deeper assessment.
